bids.reasoning: Since the negotiation is just beginning (t=0), I will start by offering my maximum utility bid to set a strong anchor. I am proposing my most preferred values for all issues, which gives me a utility of 1.0. I will monitor the opponent's counter-offers to find mutually beneficial trade-offs later.

The current time is very early in the negotiation (t=0.02) and the opponent's opening offer gives me my minimum possible utility across all issues, contradicting their message that it is a "win-win". I will disregard their cheap talk and make a microscopic concession on my lowest-weighted issue (issueA) to demonstrate flexibility and test their preferences, while remaining firm on my highest-priority issues (issueC and issueD).

The opponent is repeating their opening offer which gives me the absolute minimum utility on all issues, while claiming it is a balanced "win-win." Since it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.04), I will ignore the opponent's cheap talk and stubbornly low offers. I will make a very minor concession on my lowest-priority issue, issueA, to signal a willingness to move, while keeping my high-priority issues firmly at their maximum utility values.

The opponent is aggressively holding onto an initial offer that provides minimal utility for me across all issues (all valueA), despite their messages falsely claiming it creates a balanced "win-win outcome." Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.06), I will hold my ground on my most heavily weighted issues (issueC and issueD) while making a minor concession on issueB (from valueF to valueE) to see if they are willing to shift from their rigid stance.

We are still very early in the negotiation (t=0.08). The opponent has repeatedly offered their maximum-value position (valueA for all issues) while sending generic, recycled messages claiming to be cooperative. Since the opponent is not making any real concessions, I will make only a small adjustment to explore their preferences, conceding slightly on issueD (from valueF to valueE) while maintaining a strong total utility for myself.

The opponent has repeatedly proposed the exact same offer (all values A) while sending messages claiming it's a balanced "sweet spot." Since their behavior contradicts this and they are holding firm at my worst possible values, I will make only a very small concession to demonstrate my willingness to negotiate. I am targeting a utility of around 0.77 by slightly lowering my expectation on my least important issue (issueA) while holding firm on my most important ones (issueC and issueD).

The opponent has finally shown a slight concession on issueC, moving away from valueA. To reward this momentum while still protecting my high-importance items, I will make a small concession on issueC (dropping from valueF to valueC), which drops my utility slightly but remains highly favorable to me. I will maintain my current positions on the other issues since it is still very early in the negotiation.

The negotiation is in its early stages (t=0.14), and the opponent has just started making minor concessions on issueC (moving from valueA to valueB), while still demanding valueA for all other issues. To reciprocate, I will make a small concession on my lowest-priority issue, issueA, moving to valueB, while holding firm on the more critical issues (C and D) where maintaining high utility is essential. I will continue to ignore their boilerplate messages about "sweet spots" and "balanced outcomes" since their actual offers heavily favor their side.

The opponent has made a minor concession on issueC but remains very firm on their opening values over all other issues, despite their messages claiming a balanced approach. Since my importance weight on issueA is the lowest (0.1), I will concede entirely to their preferred valueA on issueA to signal cooperation. I will hold firm on issues B, C, and D to maintain a high utility of 0.728, hoping yielding on my lowest-priority issue prompts them to make equivalent concessions.

The current time is early (t=0.18), but the opponent has held firm on their latest offer for several rounds. I have already completely conceded my lowest-weight issue (issueA) to their preferred value to demonstrate my willingness to cooperate. To break the deadlock while protecting my core interests in issueC and issueD, I will make a modest concession on issueD down to my third-best value (valueD), bringing my overall target utility down slightly to ~0.68, to encourage the opponent to start compromising on issueB or issueD.

The opponent has made a significant concession on issueC by moving to `valueD`. Since `valueD` still offers me high utility (0.79), I will accept their proposal on this issue to encourage agreement. To maintain my overall utility near ~0.68 early in the negotiation (t=0.20), I will adjust my request on issueD back to `valueE`, engaging in a logrolling strategy to find a mutually acceptable package.

The opponent has recently pivoted, withdrawing their concession on issueC to offer a concession on issueA (valueB). Since issueA has my lowest importance (0.1), I will accept their proposal for issueA: valueB, but I will return to issueC: valueD, which they previously offered at t=0.20. I will maintain my high-utility values on my heavier-weighted issues (issueB and issueD) to keep my overall utility healthy (around 0.69) for this early stage of the negotiation (t=0.22).

The opponent has offered `issueC: valueC` and `issueA: valueA`, which I will accept in this bid as `valueC` perfectly aligns well with my utility while `issueA` represents an inexpensive concession. To maintain a fair balanced utility around 0.68, I will hold moderately firm on `issueB` and drop slightly on `issueD` to `valueD` to signal further willingness to find a middle ground as we move into the mid-game.

The opponent has recently made piecemeal concessions on issueB (valueB) and issueC (valueC). Since both of these values still offer me reasonable to excellent utility, I will incorporate them both into this offer while maintaining my position on my most heavily weighted priority, issueD (valueE). Given the early stage of the negotiation (t=0.26), this proposal sustains a strong utility for me (around 0.69) while moving toward common ground on the issues where the opponent has signaled flexibility.

I am still in the relatively early phase (t=0.28). The opponent has shown some flexibility across all issues (offering B on issueB, C on issueC, and recently C on issueD). To capitalize on these signals, I will adopt their past concessions on issueA and issueB, propose valueC for issueC (which gives me high utility and which they have previously offered), but push for my top utility value on issueD, maintaining a solid overall utility of ~0.70.

The opponent has actively probed alternative values on several isolated issues, suggesting they might accept `valueD` for `issueB` (as offered at t=0.30) and `valueC` for `issueC` (as offered at t=0.24). I will incorporate both of those values to show responsiveness to their signals, while conceding completely on `issueA` (my least important issue) to their preferred `valueA`. Since it is still relatively early (t=0.3), I will hold firm near my top utility for `issueD`, which is my heaviest-weighted issue, by offering `valueE`.

The opponent has just signaled a concession on issueD by offering `valueD`, which provides me with high utility (0.80). I will incorporate this concession alongside their previous concession on issueA (`valueB`), while holding firm on `issueC` at `valueC` and `issueB` at `valueE` to protect my core utility priorities. This keeps my overall utility around 0.69, comfortably above my reservation value while actively integrating their proposed middle grounds early in the mid-game.

The time is at t=0.34, representing the mid-game phase. The opponent recently offered `valueB` for `issueA` and has historically offered `valueB` for `issueB` and `valueC` for `issueC`. I will accommodate them by offering `valueB` on `issueA` and `issueB`, and `valueC` on `issueC`, while anchoring on `valueE` for my high-priority `issueD`. This combination targets an overall utility near 0.7, yielding to the opponent using their previously signaled acceptable values while steadily pacing my concessions well before the deadline.

I noticed the opponent has begun exploring compromises across different issues, most recently testing `valueB` on `issueA` and `valueC` on `issueB`. Since my preference weight for `issueA` and `issueB` is relatively low, I am willing to incorporate these values into my offer to signal cooperation. However, because we are still in the mid-game (t=0.36), I will hold firm on `issueC` and `issueD` to protect my highest-weighted priorities and maintain a high overall utility.

The opponent has made significant concessions on issueA and issueC, aligning issueC with my highly valued 'valueC' in their last offer. I will accept their proposed values for issueA ('valueB') and issueC ('valueC') to solidify this progress. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.38), I will incorporate their previous concession on issueB ('valueD' from t=0.30) while maintaining my firm stance on issueD ('valueE') to keep my overall utility around 0.70.

The negotiation is at t = 0.40. I will maintain high utility on issues C and D, as they carry the highest importance (0.3 weight each), where my utility targets remain `valueC` and `valueE`. I see the opponent has proposed `valueC` for `issueB` and `valueB` for `issueA`. To move toward agreement, I will adopt their preferred values on `issueB` and `issueA` (offering `valueA` which they aggressively targeted early on) while holding firm on my priorities to keep my total utility around 0.68.

The opponent has signaled flexibility on issueC and issueD in their latest bid, offering `valueD` for issueC. I am willing to meet them there on issueC since they've offered it multiple times recently, while also conceding to their preferred `valueA` on issueA. In exchange, I need to keep issueD closer to my high-utility values (`valueE`). I have incorporated their previous proposal of `valueC` on issueB to create an appealing mixed-value package that keeps my utility above 0.64.

The opponent asserts they are making concessions based on "sweet spots" and has shown flexibility on issues B, C, and D by offering them multiple times at various intermediate values, most recently `issueC: valueC` which sits nicely at 0.93 utility for me. Since we are around t=0.44, I will solidify our agreement on `issueA: valueA` and `issueC: valueC`, while attempting a fair exchange on `issueB: valueD` and `issueD: valueD`—both of which the opponent has signaled they might tolerate in past offers—targeting an overall utility around 0.64.

The time fraction is 0.46, nearing the midpoint. The opponent's last offer included a concession on issueB (valueB) and issueD (valueC). I will match their proposal on issueB (valueB) to demonstrate flexibility and signal cooperation, as this doesn't drastically harm my utility. However, since my preferences are heavily weighted toward issueC and issueD, I will hold firm on high-utility values (valueC and valueE respectively) to ensure my overall utility remains strong (approx. 0.69). Their messages remain generic and don't provide reliable directives, so I rely on tracking their bid fluctuations.

The opponent is consistently starting to mix up their bids, presenting concessions on `issueB` (offered `valueB`, `valueC`, `valueD`), `issueC` (offered `valueC`, `valueD`, `valueE`), and `issueD`. We are approaching the mid-point (t=0.48), so I aim for a solid utility around 0.70. I will adopt their previously floated values on my lower-weight issues (e.g., `valueB` for `issueA` and `issueB`, and `valueC` for `issueC`) while holding relatively firm on `issueD` (`valueE`), which has a high weight in my preferences.

I have observed that the opponent favors `valueA` and `valueB` for `issueB`, and they frequently return to `valueE` and `valueA` on some of the other issues. I will attempt to slowly yield on `issueA` and `issueB` (which are lower importance to me) to accommodate their preferences while standing firm on `issueC` and `issueD` to maintain my utility around 0.71, as there is still half the negotiation time remaining.

The negotiation is halfway through the timeline, and the opponent has consistently signaled flexibility with varied values across multiple issues. To demonstrate strong cooperation, I am directly adopting three of the four proposed values from their latest offer (issueA: valueB, issueB: valueC, issueC: valueD). In exchange for this significant concession on my part, I am holding firm on issueD at valueE to ensure the overall package remains at a viable utility level (0.65) for me. This moves us extremely close to a final deal.

The opponent's most recent offer provided substantial concessions on `issueB` (valueC) and `issueD` (valueE), which they explicitly emphasized in their message. Since we are around the mid-point (t = 0.54), I am willing to meet them on those specific values, as well as on their preferred `valueA` for `issueA`. To maintain my utility above 0.6, I will reinstate `valueD` for `issueC`, a condition they showed willingness to accept very recently (t = 0.52). Combining these creates a highly defensible compromise package.

The opponent's most recent offers featured issueA: valueD and issueC: valueD at t=0.56, as well as issueB: valueC and issueD: valueE at t=0.54. By combining these specific component values, I am assembling an offer comprised entirely of terms the opponent has recently proposed, demonstrating a willingness to meet their interests. At the same time, this combination yields a very strong total utility of 0.684 for myself, making it an optimal compromise for the mid-point of the negotiation.

I am targeting a utility around 0.74 as we enter the mid-to-late negotiation phase. I noticed you just offered `valueF` for `issueB` (my top utility for that issue), so I will accept that concession. To reciprocate, I am offering `valueA` on `issueA` which you frequently demand, while locking in `valueD` for `issueC` and `valueE` for `issueD`, both of which you have recently signaled as acceptable in previous rounds.

The time is at t=0.60, entering the mid-to-late stage of negotiation. The opponent has signaled willingness to concede by offering `valueF` for issueB (t=0.58) and `valueE` for issueD (t=0.60) in recent turns, both of which are very high-utility items for me. To build on this momentum, I will combine their recent concessions (`issueA=valueB`, `issueB=valueF`, `issueD=valueE`) and pair it with `issueC=valueC` to propose a strong mutual agreement while preserving a high utility (~0.79) for myself.

To maximize my utility while responding to the opponent's recent signals, I will hold firm on my highest-weight issues (issueC and issueD) by demanding values that offer me substantial utility (valueC and valueE, respectively), as the opponent has previously shown flexibility on these. Simultaneously, I recognize the opponent's recent willingness to concede issueB to valueF, which is my top preference, so I will incorporate that. For issueA, a lower-weight issue for me, I will offer valueA to match their historical preference.

The opponent's most recent offer includes `issueC: valueF`, which corresponds to my highest-utility value for that heavily weighted issue, while they have previously proposed `issueA: valueA`, `issueB: valueF`, and `issueD: valueE` in other turns. To capitalize on this, I will construct a bid merging these elements. This provides me with a highly favorable utility (surpassing 0.8) while remaining composed solely of values the opponent has recently floated as acceptable.

The opponent significantly conceded on issueB (offering valueF, our top choice) and previously offered valueF on issueC. While their latest offer drops to utility 0.604, we have reached the mid-to-late phase of bargaining (t=0.66). To encourage an agreement while maximizing our gain, I will hold their concession on B:F and issueA (to accommodate them), while proposing valueF on issueC and stepping down to valueD on issueD. This secures a strong utility for us (0.760) and tests their flexibility on issueD.

I have targeted an overall utility of ~0.70 by retaining strong configurations on my heavier-weighted issues while giving some leeway on the lighter-weighted ones. The opponent's last couple of offers have included `valueB` on `issueA`, `valueF` or `valueC` on `issueB`, and `valueD` on `issueC`. To meet them in a productive direction, I am structuring a compromise combining `valueF` for `issueB` (which they previously offered) and matching their `valueD` on `issueC`.

The opponent's most recent offer gave me my preferred values on issueB (`valueF`) and a highly beneficial option on issueC (`valueC`). However, they dropped issueD strongly to `valueB`, which lowers my overall utility significantly. Since I have given ground on issueA (which they care deeply about) and the deadline is still a bit away at t=0.7, I will accept their terms on issues A, B, and C, but request a moderate concession on issueD (`valueD`) to maintain an acceptable utility near 0.74. Their messages are repetitive platitudes about balance, so I am relying strictly on the numeric shifts in their bids.

The opponent's latest proposal (A=valueF, B=valueC, C=valueA, D=valueA) shows a strong attempt to compromise on issue A but drastically reduces my utility on issues B, C, and D, taking my total utility down to 0.320. As we are entering the final quarter of the negotiation (t=0.72), I will pivot back to values that have shown some past mutual acceptability, maintaining `valueF` for B and `valueC` for C, while offering a slight concession on issue A (`valueD`) and maintaining issue D at `valueD` to target a utility near 0.78.

The negotiation is entering the late stage (t=0.74). The opponent has shown willingness to accept `valueA` on issueA, `valueF` on issueB, `valueC` on issueC, and recently proposed `valueE` for issueD. Since my weights for issueA is the lowest (0.1) and highest for issueC and issueD (0.3), I am adopting a combined bid (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueF, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueE) that gathers the best components the opponent has already signaled acceptance for, while still providing me a strong utility of ~0.78.

The opponent's message points out that we are fully aligned on issueB, which is true because they recently offered `valueF`. They have also recently offered `valueB` for issueA, `valueC` for issueC, and `valueE` for issueD in mixed combinations. Since we are moving into the final quarter of the negotiation, I will assemble these overlapping values into an offer that meets them where they have signaled acceptance while maintaining a strong utility (around 0.79) for myself by securing `valueC` on issueC and `valueE` on issueD.

As time approaches the deadline (t=0.78), I need to make calculated concessions while maintaining a strong total utility. The opponent's recent messages highlight their "significant concessions" and their t=0.74 bid proposed issueC: valueE and issueD: valueE. By offering a package that incorporates those values alongside issueA: valueA and issueB: valueF (which they have bid multiple times previously), I aim to present an attractive combination that secures me a solid utility of ~0.74 while facilitating an agreement.

The opponent's most recent offer at t=0.80 (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueF, issueC: valueE, issueD: valueF) provides me with an exceptionally high utility of 0.754. It maximizes my value on two key issues (issueD and issueB) while securing a very strong value on issueC. Given the late stage of the negotiation (t=0.8), I am choosing to accept this deal by mirroring the opponent's exact offer to guarantee agreement before the deadline.
